title: What I Gave Them
storyPurpose: Cael confesses: threats, leaks, start point, disasters never intended.

panels.description: Cael Vesper the Lanternmaster: mid-40s, warm brown skin, silver-threaded dark hair, deep blue and ember-gold coat, brass lantern clasps, half-mask of painted wood with nested lantern motif kneeling; confession without theatrics; Mira standing, hurt-controlled; Nira listening.

bubbles.text: They took Lumenhare after the market tore. Said cooperation would stop another tear.
bubbles.speaker: Cael Vesper

panels.description: Inset flashes (no text): route maps, Tempestria crest, egg token — visual timeline of leaks.

bubbles.text: I gave routes first. Then doors. Then… that you had the egg.
bubbles.speaker: Cael Vesper

bubbles.text: I never meant for anyone to die.
bubbles.speaker: Cael Vesper

panels.description: Mira Eggwarden: young adult Hatchery mentor/Keeper, warm brown skin, dark hair in practical braid, travel-stained hatchery coat over Compact robes, Compact lantern charm on satchel, determined protective eyes silence; Spark presses against Cael's knee without absolution; Aurelia cold-precise.

bubbles.text: Intent does not refund the sold road.
bubbles.speaker: Aurelia Voss

grokPrompt: Original high-energy Western fantasy comic storytelling with dynamic panel composition, dramatic inked linework, richly painted colors, expressive character acting, and clear cinematic action. Original Riftwilds IP only. Black stone, pale silver metal, moss and timber first; cyan Rift light, deep red warning runes, restrained violet gate shimmer as accents only. NO purple AI-fantasy neon default. NO Marvel/DC/Pokémon characters or logos. Riftwilds comic "The Traitor's Gate" Issue #7, STORY PAGE 14/25 — What I Gave Them. Story purpose: Cael confesses: threats, leaks, start point, disasters never intended. Layout: three-stack. 3 panels with clear inked gutters. Panel 1 (p14a): Cael Vesper the Lanternmaster: mid-40s, warm brown skin, silver-threaded dark hair, deep blue and ember-gold coat, brass lantern clasps, half-mask of painted wood with nested lantern motif kneeling; confession without theatrics; Mira standing, hurt-controlled; Nira listening. Panel 2 (p14b): Inset flashes (no text): route maps, Tempestria crest, egg token — visual timeline of leaks. Panel 3 (p14c): Mira Eggwarden: young adult Hatchery mentor/Keeper, warm brown skin, dark hair in practical braid, travel-stained hatchery coat over Compact robes, Compact lantern charm on satchel, determined protective eyes silence; Spark presses against Cael's knee without absolution; Aurelia cold-precise. Characters: mira-eggwarden, cael-vesper, nira-quill, aurelia-voss. Creatures: spark, dormant-egg. Spark design lock: Spark the Glowpup-line Riftborn hatchling: soft luminous fur, cyan-gold rift markings, large expressive eyes, small crystal growths, glowing-tip emotional tail, steadier aura when bonded; cute but original — not a franchise mascot. Keeper lock: Mira Eggwarden: young adult Hatchery mentor/Keeper, warm brown skin, dark hair in practical braid, travel-stained hatchery coat over Compact robes, Compact lantern charm on satchel, determined protective eyes. Egg lock: dormant Riftborn egg: warm oval shell with cyan-amber seam markings, soft pulse glow, no full hatch — visually identical across pages. Oathwarden lock: Oathwarden Seraph: tall ceremonial black-and-silver armor, split judgment mask, cyan oath flame, long judgment spear, broken Keeper cloak, floating lock rings, one armor side corrupted, orbiting memory mirrors. Environment: Traitor Gate approaches. Time: day. Lighting: black stone with cyan rift accents. Continuity: {"confession": "complete", "forgiveness": "not-yet"} Leave empty balloon-safe and narration-safe negative space in upper/lower panel corners. NO readable text of any kind in the artwork.
negativePrompt: readable dialogue text, captions, logos, watermarks, page numbers, UI chrome, Marvel, DC, Pokémon, manga screentone trademarks, extra limbs, duplicate characters, missing companions, purple neon fantasy default, photoreal modern clothing, Pikachu lookalike, Cal Reed, Voltkit
letteringInstructions: Standard speech + narration; keep tails off faces and Spark's eyes; Gate text formal.
artAlt: What I Gave Them — The Traitor's Gate page 14
transcript: SPEECH (Cael Vesper): They took Lumenhare after the market tore. Said cooperation would stop another tear.
SPEECH (Cael Vesper): I gave routes first. Then doors. Then… that you had the egg.
SPEECH (Cael Vesper): I never meant for anyone to die.
SPEECH (Aurelia Voss): Intent does not refund the sold road.
